t_ch: 我听着风言风语，说入侵时期有个极为骇人的怪物躲在这里，专挑平日研究成绩垫底的科员下手。
t_en: I've heard rumors of a horrific monster that sneaked in during the invasion. It is said to prey on the lowest-performing researchers.

t_ch: 嘟噜噜，想想就恐怖，我可别遇上它。
t_en: Just thinking about it gives me jitters. I hope I never cross paths with it.

t_ch: 星穹列车。
t_en: The Astral Express.

t_ch: 列车嘛，按规矩它不归空间站管。
t_en: Well, strictly speaking, the Express isn't part of the space station.

t_ch: 你是列车的人吧？那卖你个人情，一并写上！
t_en: Hey, you're one of the Express folk, right? I'll do you a favor and add its damages to the books!

t_ch: 损毁的机器人。
t_en: The broken robot.

t_ch: 流线型的机身，银白的金属光泽……
t_en: A streamlined body. A silver-white metallic luster...

t_ch: 这不是艾丝妲站长订购的机器人吗？不得不说…她的眼光真不错！
t_en: Is this the robot that Asta ordered? I have to admit... She has great taste!

t_ch: 这些够了吗？
t_en: Are these enough?

t_ch: 说多不多，说少不少。我统统记在账册上了，你确认一下。
t_en: More or less. I've put it all in the ledger, you can take a look now.

t_ch: 看完了吗？眼睛别乱瞟，跟你有关的就这几行。没什么问题的话，照片我就拿走了。
t_en: Are you done yet? Don't just nose around. There are only a few lines that concern you. And if there's nothing wrong, I'll take the photos.

t_ch: 好家伙，几千项货损。
t_en: There are thousands of items on that list!

t_ch: 都确有其事嘛，而且「公司」报销，咱也不跟他们客气。
t_en: You don't say! And IPC will reimburse every one of them. There's no need to be coy about it.

t_ch: 感觉这清单怪怪的。
t_en: It seems like there's something odd about that list.

t_ch: 有什么怪的？都是「公司」报销，坏了就是坏了，咱也不跟他们客气。
t_en: Odd? IPC will reimburse us for everything, there's no need to be coy about it with them.

t_ch: 世齐千里镜？
t_en: Shiqi Megascope?

t_ch: 对呀，虽然只磕了个角，那也是磕了。反正都是「公司」报销，咱也不跟他们客气。
t_en: Yes, even though it only got bumped slightly in the corner, it still counts as damage. In any case, IPC will reimburse us for everything, so there's no need to be coy about it.

t_ch: 这几千项货损，不仔细看，谁会发现啊。
t_en: With the thousands of things listed here, who's going to know the difference unless they check every one?

t_ch: 好啦那我先整理账册啰，晚点交回科里。你也早点休息，不送~♪
t_en: Okay, I'll sort out the ledger for submission later. You should go get some rest.

t_ch: {F#丫头}{M#小子}你动作还挺快，这边几个大箱子里好东西不少，你自己找找啊。
t_en: You move pretty quick, {F#lass}{M#lad}. There are lots of nice things in these boxes here. Help yourself.

t_ch: 那个我有点急事，要先走啰！
t_en: I am in a hurry. Bye!

t_ch: 这开关你为什么不自己打开？
t_en: Why didn't you just turn on the switch yourself?

t_ch: 为什么不自己打开…嚯嚯，兴许是我这把老骨头没注意到吧。
t_en: Why didn't I turn it on myself... Hoho, I suppose I didn't notice.

t_ch: 你刚刚答应我的天价报酬呢？
t_en: What about the big reward you promised me?

t_ch: 什么天价报酬…我怎么不记得我答应过？
t_en: What a big reward... I don't remember making such a promise.

t_ch: 好蹩脚的演技…
t_en: Such terrible acting...

t_ch: 啥？我听不清，{F#丫头}{M#小子}，你大点声！
t_en: What? I can't hear you clearly, {F#lass}{M#lad}. Speak up!

t_ch: …算了，你没事就好。
t_en: ... Forget it, as long as you're fine.

t_ch: 我当然没事！浑身上下，每一块骨头，都好着哪。
t_en: Of course I'm fine! Every muscle and bone, everything is fine.

t_ch: 你就是想吸引我的注意。
t_en: So you just wanted to get my attention.

t_ch: 我不是，我没有。{F#丫头}{M#小子}，你可别乱说啊。
t_en: No. I did not. Don't be ridiculous, {F#lass}{M#lad}.

t_ch: 你不会是想碰瓷吧…
t_en: You're not trying to blackmail me... are you?

t_ch: 我又没有讹你，怎么能说是碰瓷呢？顶多占用了一点你的注意力。
t_en: I didn't ask for anything! How can you call this blackmail? At most I just took up a little of your time.

t_ch: 快快快，让开让开，我憋不住了。有事到机修间找我！
t_en: Come on, get out of the way, I can't hold it anymore. Come get me in the maintenance room if you need anything!

t_ch: 你看，这汽水起子也不知道扔在零件箱里多久了，总算给我找出来啰！
t_en: Look, I don't know for how long this bottle-opener has been in the toolbox. I finally found it!

t_ch: 我手上这瓶，可是四个琥珀纪前的汽水<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>，历久弥香，嗬…爽快！
t_en: This bottle of soda is from four Amber Eras ago, <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color>, so fragrant. Ho... so refreshing!

t_ch: 怎么样，要不要来一口？
t_en: Would you like a sip?

t_ch: 来一口来一口！
t_en: Just one sip!

t_ch: 妙啊，年轻人！你是不知道刚刚我有多烦躁，手上拿着好东西，却只能望梅止渴，差点我就上牙咬了。
t_en: Good one, kid! You have no idea how frustrated I was just now. I had this tasty treat in my hand, but I couldn't drink it! I almost tried to pry it open with my teeth!

t_ch: 也怪我自己，清理轴承阻塞，还非把瓶子揣上；瓶子揣上就算了，把起子忘了。
t_en: It's my own fault for bringing it with me when I went down to unblock those bearings. Why did I forget the bottle opener?

t_ch: 气早就没了吧。
t_en: Must've gone flat ages ago, right?

t_ch: 气足着呢，要说我在你这年纪，就凭这一口气，点了古恩的胡子，撮合伯纳德和密卷科那娘们儿……
t_en: I haven't gone flat! When I was your age, I could light Gunn's beard on fire and set up Bernard with that chick in the Department of Insight, all in one go!

t_ch: 只可惜飞跃「黑塔」这事，我还真差一口气。
t_en: Sadly, I really was a bit flat in the "Station Breakout" incident.

t_ch: 时间也过得真快，我现在连汽水起子都忘了带，不得不服老了。
t_en: Time flies, though. Now, I even forget to bring a bottle opener. I can't deny I am old.

t_ch: 是这种憋不住…
t_en: So this is why you were in a hurry...

t_ch: 嗬，那还能是啥？我辛辛苦苦处理阻塞，奖励自己一瓶汽水不很正常么？
t_en: Ha, what did you expect? Can't I reward myself with a bottle of soda after working so hard dealing with that blockage?

t_ch: 就是忘了带汽水起子，哎，这种事上还是不得不服老啊。
t_en: I just forgot to bring the opener. Sigh... I must admit I am old.

t_ch: 我告诉你啊，这<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>曾经可是空间站的专利，风靡星系的反重力饮品，我手上的存货就剩这最后几瓶了。
t_en: Let me tell you, the patent for this <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color> used to belong to the space station. This anti-gravity drink was once all the rage across the cosmos. I only have a few bottles left.

t_ch: 喝一口少一口，可惜啊，可惜！
t_en: I'd better enjoy every last sip while I still can.

t_ch: 啊对了？！还没自我介绍吧？
t_en: Oh, right! I haven't introduced myself yet, have I?

t_ch: 老爹我啊，是这儿的维修师温天翁。活得最久，资历最老，手也最稳——
t_en: I am Wen Tianweng, the mechanic. I am number one around here in terms of age, experience, and skills.

t_ch: 可惜屁用没有，整个机修间就我一个人。
t_en: Unfortunately, it doesn't mean jack, because I'm the only one in the maintenance room.

t_ch: 曾经倒有过一个同伙，可惜是个恶棍，不仅曾把空间站炸出大洞，还经常遣回古怪的信件，三天两头阻塞轴承，惹我前去修理。
t_en: I had a partner once, but he was a scoundrel. Not only did he blow a big hole in the space station, but he also kept sending back these strange letters. He always blocked the bearings and made me go repair them.

t_ch: 我和那只会给人添麻烦的恶棍，注定八字不合。背了阿基维利的大运，才会卡在那儿。
t_en: I never got along with that troublemaker. It's as if we were fated to be at odds with each other... Ah, Akivili must've turned their back on me, that's why I got stuck in there.

t_ch: 卡了老半天，可把我憋坏啰，哎哟哟。
t_en: I was stuck for so long it was starting to ache. Ouch!

t_ch: 你在下面答应给我报酬…
t_en: You promised to pay me...

t_ch: 报酬？你看我像是有钱的样子吗？
t_en: Pay? Do I look rich to you?

t_ch: 我只是个穷困潦倒的维修工，一直为恶棍搞出的大洞背负着「渎职」的罪名……
t_en: I'm just a poor mechanic. I've been charged with dereliction of duty ever since that scoundrel made that giant hole in the station.

t_ch: 你要一般的报酬，我没有，呵呵……
t_en: If you want a reward, I don't have any, hehe...

t_ch: 不过！既然和你说了半天，也算投缘，值得破例一回。
t_en: However! Since we've been talking this whole time and you seem agreeable enough, it's worth making an exception.

t_ch: 下面的话你可听好啦。我要给你的是……
t_en: Listen carefully now. What I am going to give you is...

t_ch: 一百个宝箱？
t_en: A hundred treasure chests?

t_ch: 十张星轨专票？
t_en: Ten Star Rail Special Passes?

t_ch: 去去，你这什么跟什么啊。老爹是要给你来一招仙人指路，保你飞来横财。
t_en: Huh... what are you on about? I am only going to show you the way to make money. You are guaranteed to make a fortune.

t_ch: 而这横财的来路嘛，首先要从一个人开始说起。
t_en: As for the origin of this fortune... well, it all started with this person.

t_ch: 还记得刚刚我提到的恶棍吗？他的真名叫做<color=#dbc291ff>何塞•拉萨罗</color>。
t_en: Remember the scoundrel I was just talking about? His real name is <color=#dbc291ff>Hosea Lazaro</color>.

t_ch: 这老斑鸠有过不少唬人的头衔。比如<color=#dbc291ff>「前地概科员」、「前无名客」、「武装考古学派成员」</color>。
t_en: That old fart has a bunch of grand titles, like <color=#dbc291ff>former researcher in the Department of Cosmic Geography, former member of the Nameless, and member of the Armed Archaeologists</color>.

t_ch: 他还在空间站的时候，就整天夸耀自己发明的<color=#dbc291ff>勘星钎、星域导影图、特殊显影法、各种软饮专利</color>有的没的，恃才傲物，谁也不服。
t_en: When he was still in the space station, he would boast about his inventions all day. <color=#dbc291ff>Astro Rod, Cosmos Guide chart, the Special Autoradiograph, various soft drink patents.</color> He was so arrogant and refused to obey anyone.

t_ch: 而且，他尤其看不惯地概科员受人欺负，经常为本科室出头，和别人大打出手。
t_en: What's more, he would get very upset whenever he saw his colleagues from the Department of Cosmic Geography being bullied by others. He would often stand up for his colleagues and start fights.

t_ch: 的确是个恶棍…
t_en: What a scoundrel...

t_ch: 哼，没错，难为你也发现了这底下的真相。
t_en: Humph, that's right. I am glad you can see the truth.

t_ch: 听起来是号人物。
t_en: Sounds like a decent guy.

t_ch: 拉倒吧，也只有你们这种小年轻，会被那些虚张声势的头衔和发明给唬住。
t_en: Yeah, right. Only impressionable young kids like you would get intimidated by those pretentious titles and inventions.

t_ch: 麻烦快些说重点？
t_en: Could you get to the point?

t_ch: 年轻人，就是性子急。唠嗑就讲究一个慢字，再说了，我哪句话不是重点？
t_en: Kids these days are always so impatient. The key to having good conversations is to let things sink in. Besides, which one of my words is not on point?

t_ch: 想要宝贝啊，你就得听我跟你慢慢唠。
t_en: If you want the treasure, you have to listen to me talk first.

t_ch: 总而言之，何塞那家伙是出了名的「动手能力」强。
t_en: Long story short, Hosea is famous for his craftsmanship.

t_ch: 这一方面，是因为他自己喜欢私下鼓捣；另一方面，是他几乎天天到机修间来，跟我学上两手。
t_en: On one hand, it's because he likes to tinker with things in his spare time. On the other hand, it's because he would come to the maintenance room to learn from me almost every day.

t_ch: 他负责疏通阻塞、发明超镍零件，还有冲调<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>。我嘛，则负责监督他干活…对了，我俩还合写过一本《汽水与空间站维修艺术》。
t_en: He was responsible for clearing blockages. He invented Ultra Nickel Parts, and he brewed Kapo-Kali. As for me... I was responsible for supervising his work... By the way, we also wrote a book together, The Art of Soda and Space Station Maintenance.

t_ch: 是有过一段快乐的时光…啊，是有过一段快乐的时光。
t_en: We did have a good time... yes... it really was quite good.

t_ch: 直到有一天，我正睡着呢，突然被一声巨响吵醒——
t_en: Until one day, I was suddenly woken up from my sleep by a terribly loud noise —

t_ch: 那恶棍竟然把机修间炸出一个大洞，乘着飞行器扬长而去！给我留下一地的烂摊子。
t_en: That scoundrel blew a giant hole in the maintenance room and flew away in an aircraft! Leaving me with all the mess.

t_ch: 后来我还是从别人口中得知，他那进行星际旅行的飞行器机油，还是从我这机修间一点一点偷去的！
t_en: Afterward, I learned from the others that the fuel in the spacecraft that he used for his adventure was stolen bit by bit from my maintenance room!

t_ch: 这就是他四处宣扬的「飞跃黑塔」事件。多少年了，这事我一想起来，就气得牙痒痒。
t_en: And this is the "Station Breakout" incident that he boasts about all over the place. It's been so many years, but whenever I think about it, I'm still so mad at him.

t_ch: 卧薪尝胆酷毙哉。
t_en: He endured hardship to accomplish his ambition. That's so cool.

t_ch: 少来，你以为「飞跃黑塔」很风光？
t_en: Oh come on. You think the "Station Breakout" is something to be proud of?

t_ch: 这也就是第一次被何塞•拉萨罗钻了空子，打那以后，有点火星儿，就会拉响全站警报。
t_en: And that was the first time Hosea Lazaro exploited us. After that, whenever there is a tiny spark, the whole station would go up in alarm.

t_ch: 你要是学他，我保证你一只脚还没离地，就会被防卫科的小崽子们按在地上一顿摩擦。
t_en: If you try to copy him, I guarantee that those kids from the Security Department will mop the floor with you before you even lift a foot off the ground.

t_ch: 破坏公物真过分。
t_en: That's destruction of public property. It's unforgivable.

t_ch: 是啊，放着好好的月台不走，非要在我的地盘闹事。
t_en: That's right. He just wanted to cause trouble on my turf.

t_ch: 他倒是一走了之，这大洞欠下的巨额债务，我一股脑背到了今天。
t_en: He just walked away, but to this very day, I am still paying the huge debt from all the damage he's done.

t_ch: 背叛师友不道德。
t_en: He betrayed his teacher and friend.

t_ch: 算你有良心！千万别有样学样，着了何塞•拉萨罗的道。
t_en: I am glad to hear you have a conscience after all! Never be like that Hosea Lazaro.

t_ch: 我可听说，地概科现在有不少年轻人，把那老斑鸠当作精神偶像，真是不学好。
t_en: I've been told there are many young people in the Department of Cosmic Geography who worship that old scoundrel like a spiritual idol. What a bunch of buffoons.

t_ch: 长篇大论快睡着…
t_en: Such a long speech. I am falling asleep...

t_ch: …扯远了，扯远了。唉，上了年纪，就是有这追忆从前的毛病。
t_en: ...I went off-topic. Eh... that's what happens when you get old.

t_ch: 平时没人说话，现在一股脑全倒出来，浑身上下舒坦多了——我真正想给你的东西，正是何塞•拉萨罗留下的一张<color=#dbc291ff>藏宝图</color>。
t_en: Usually, I have no one to talk to, but now that I got to let it all out, I feel much better. So, what I really want to give you is the <color=#dbc291ff>Treasure Map</color> that Hosea Lazaro left behind.

t_ch: 这么好的东西不早拿出来。
t_en: Awesome. You should have shown me earlier.

t_ch: 找人唠嗑才是你的目的吧…
t_en: You just wanted someone to chat with, didn't you...

t_ch: 才不是，瞎说八道什么哪。
t_en: Of course not. Stop talking nonsense.

t_ch: 这东西藏在空间站的犄角旮旯，被不相干的人翻了出来，以为是废纸。
t_en: This thing was hidden in a corner somewhere in the space station. Someone found it and thought it was trash.

t_ch: 但辗转到老爹我手上，用那老斑鸠发明的特殊显影法一试，嚯！发现上面揭示了某个房间的坐标。
t_en: But it ended up here with me, I tried using that old scoundrel's special technique to reveal the text, and lo and behold! It revealed the coordinates of a room.

t_ch: 听说那恶棍曾在空间站留下不少私房宝贝，想必都藏在这里。
t_en: I've been told that the old scoundrel left many personal treasures in the space station. They must be hidden there.

t_ch: 你尽管按照图上的位置去找，只要你能找得到，东西可都归你！
t_en: You can look for them according to the location on the map. If you can find them, then everything belongs to you!

t_ch: 我得再去整两口<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>了，配上咔滋闪光的焊枪，就是阿基维利来了我也不换！
t_en: I have to go take a couple of sips of <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color> and tinker around with my blazing welding gun. I wouldn't give up on that even if Akivili were to come pay me a visit!

t_ch: 找着了？是些啥？
t_en: Did you find them? What are these?

t_ch: 别误会，我就问问，我反正是不信老斑鸠能留下什么好东西。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. I'm just curious. I don't believe that old scoundrel would leave us anything good.

t_ch: 你手上是什么纸…啊！是黑塔大人…大不敬，大不敬啊！快给我，别让其他人看见了。
t_en: What's the paper you are holding...? Oh! That's Madam Herta... Disrespectful, really disrespectful! Give it to me and don't let others see it.

t_ch: 我要收藏…
t_en: I want to keep it...

t_ch: 你别是被耍了？我就知道，狗改不了吃屎，何塞•拉萨罗也转不了性。
t_en: Don't be fooled! A tiger never changes its stripes. You can't trust that scoundrel Hosea.

t_ch: 快给我，要是被别人发现，我们一老一少都跑不了。
t_en: Quick! Give it to me. If someone finds out, we are done for.

t_ch: 显影看看？
t_en: Try the Autoradiograph?

t_ch: 怎么，这「宝藏」上面也标注着「宝藏」的位置？在这儿跟老爹我玩套娃哪？
t_en: What? This treasure is also marked with the location of another treasure? What game is he playing?

t_ch: 给我瞧瞧。
t_en: Let me have a look.

t_ch: 什么，这是封加密信…<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>…的珍贵秘方？！
t_en: What? This is an encrypted letter... the secret recipe of... <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color>!?

t_ch: 好家伙！等着啊，显影试剂管够——
t_en: Why you little! Just you wait. There's enough Autoradiograph reagent—

t_ch: 搞什么名堂啊，这老斑鸠。
t_en: What's he playing at, that old scoundrel.

t_ch: 找到宝藏就好。
t_en: As long as we can find the treasure.

t_ch: 焦糖，二氧化碳，听起来挺耳熟。香涎…是口水的意思吗？！
t_en: Caramel, carbon dioxide, sounds familiar. Ambergris... Does it mean saliva!?

t_ch: 还算他讲点义气。活到这份上，我就念着这口<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>了。
t_en: At least he has a shred of loyalty left. At my age, a sip of <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color> is the only thing I want...

t_ch: 你就原谅他吧。
t_en: Just forgive him already.

t_ch: 行了，都是糟老头子之间的事，年轻人瞎掺和什么。
t_en: Enough. This is between us two old farts. Young people shouldn't get involved.

t_ch: 而且，说到底，我记恨他，那都是气话。
t_en: And besides, when I said I hated him, I said it in the heat of the moment.

t_ch: 能送到这封信也不容易。
t_en: It wasn't easy to deliver this letter.

t_ch: 对不住啊，{F#丫头}{M#小子}。我没想到他会唱上这么一出。
t_en: Sorry, {F#lass}{M#lad}. I didn't expect that from him.

t_ch: …我记恨他，那也都是…气话！哎！
t_en: When I said I hated him, I said it in the heat of the moment! Argh!

t_ch: 其实，老爹我从来都不在乎他给我造成的损失。
t_en: Of course, I never cared about all the mess he left me with.

t_ch: 打从小时候，我就不喜欢学术研究和应物科那档子破事儿。
t_en: Ever since I was young, I have never liked academic research and dealing with the Department of Implement Arts.

t_ch: 我最大的愿望，就是闷头躲在这机修间，拧拧螺丝，动动扳手。醒了就干，累了就睡。在我眼里，和机器打交道，可比跟人打交道快乐多了……
t_en: My biggest wish was to hide in this maintenance room and play with the tools. Work when I wake up and sleep when I get tired. In my opinion, dealing with machines is much more fun than dealing with people...

t_ch: 但这么多年空间站人来人往，唯独何塞•拉萨罗是个例外。
t_en: But so many people have come and gone through this space station over the years, with the exception of Hosea.

t_ch: 所以我生气的是，这老斑鸠没把自己的逃亡计划漏给我，哪怕一丁点儿。
t_en: So, I was angry that the old scoundrel didn't share his breakout plan with me. Not even a little bit.

t_ch: 我们不是兄弟吗？兄弟不该无话不谈吗？
t_en: Aren't we brothers? Shouldn't brothers be able to talk about anything?

t_ch: …不过，那么多年过去，我早就释怀了。
t_en: ...Nevertheless, it's been so many years. I have long since learned to let it go.

t_ch: 相反，每次听到他在外面「为非作歹」的消息，我都真心替他感到高兴。
t_en: On the contrary, whenever I hear about what kind of trouble he's making outside, I really do feel happy for him.

t_ch: 他天生就适合那片广阔银河。适合不要命地使劲折腾。
t_en: He was born to run amok all over the cosmos.

t_ch: 你活得挺通透。
t_en: You are very enlightened.

t_ch: 和自己较劲多累啊。
t_en: One has got to learn to let it go eventually.

t_ch: 他也把你当兄弟。
t_en: He also thinks of you as a brother.

t_ch: 或许吧。但「飞跃黑塔」这事他要真叫上我，我还不一定够胆呢。
t_en: Maybe. But if he had really invited me to make a breakout from the space station, I may not have had the guts for it.

t_ch: 我也喜欢折腾。
t_en: I like to run amok too.

t_ch: 你还年轻着呢，以后有的是机会。不是折腾这个世界，就是被这个世界折腾。
t_en: You are still young. There will be plenty of opportunities in the future. Either you run amok all over the world or it runs all over you.

t_ch: 所以，兜了这么一大圈，<color=#dbc291ff>「宝藏」</color>就是这张配方，{F#丫头}{M#小子}，你怎么说？
t_en: So, after all that... The <color=#dbc291ff>Treasure</color> is just this recipe, {F#lass}{M#lad}. What do you think?

t_ch: 我想拿去换钱。
t_en: I want to exchange it for money.

t_ch: 虽说想法不错，但这张手稿已经被显影试剂浸透，脆弱不堪，一碰即损，怕是撑不到你与买家接头的那一天。
t_en: As good as the idea may sound, this manuscript is soaked in Autoradiopraph reagent, which makes it very fragile. You'll break it with the slightest touch. I'm afraid it won't last until you find a buyer.

t_ch: 让我想想，不如给你些别处寻不到的珍贵货。看手稿上所写这些香料，在仓库里耐心找找不成问题……
t_en: Let me see. I guess I can give you a few treasures you won't find anywhere else. And I may have some of these ingredients in the warehouse.

t_ch: 留给你作纪念吧。
t_en: You keep it, as a memento.

t_ch: 老爹我才不稀罕呢！除非你坚持，我才考虑勉为其难把它收下。
t_en: I do NOT want it! Unless you insist, then maybe I'll consider taking it home.

t_ch: 它对你很重要。
t_en: It is very important to you.

t_ch: 既然这样，那我就给你露一手。
t_en: Well, in that case, let me show you something.

t_ch: 让我看看，这些香料，在仓库里耐心找找不成问题……
t_en: Let me see, these ingredients... I suppose I can find some in the warehouse if I look hard enough...

t_ch: 那我留着了。
t_en: I will keep it then.

t_ch: 等等！我就是客气一下，你怎么还当真了呢……
t_en: Hold on! I was just being polite. What were you thinking...?

t_ch: 这样吧，手稿我留着，给你露一手别的功夫。
t_en: How about this. I'll keep the manuscript, and I'll show you something else.

t_ch: 我想要点别的。
t_en: I want something else.

t_ch: 没办法了，老斑鸠遛了你这么久，结果这债还是要我来还。
t_en: Well... then I suppose there's no other choice. That old scoundrel... after all these years, I am still paying off his debt.

t_ch: 既然这样，那我就给你露一手。
t_en: Well, in that case, let me show you something.

t_ch: 让我看看，这些香料，在仓库里耐心找找不成问题……
t_en: Let me see, these ingredients... I suppose I can find some in the warehouse if I look hard enough...

t_ch: 来啰来啰，一大桶新鲜灌装的<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>…就是这个味道！
t_en: Come on, a big bucket of fresh <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color>... That's the taste!

t_ch: 这些汽水你就收下吧，提神醒脑，健体强身。
t_en: Just take these sodas with you. They can refresh your mind and strengthen your body.

t_ch: 何塞•拉萨罗离开之后，也就你这{F#丫头}{M#小子}，愿意跟我这么折腾。
t_en: {F#Lass}{M#Lad}, you're the only one who would talk to me ever since Hosea left.

t_ch: 你要是对那老斑鸠感兴趣，可以再在空间站里找找，还有没有他寄回来的天外信件。上面写满了自吹自擂。
t_en: If you are interested in that old scoundrel, you can look around the space station again and see if you can find some of those letters he sent back. Those letters are filled with his bragging.

t_ch: 他啊…可是巴不得自己的废话能被更多人听见。
t_en: That guy... just wants more people to hear his nonsense.

t_ch: 老年人是这样的。
t_en: Old people are like that.

t_ch: 废话也挺有意思。
t_en: Nonsense can be very interesting to listen to.

t_ch: 哈哈！你这{F#丫头}{M#小子}……
t_en: Haha! You little {F#brat}{M#brat}...

t_ch: 我也和你唠得差不多了。以后要是没什么事……
t_en: Alright, enough chitchat. From now on, whenever you are free...

t_ch: 哈，记得多来陪我这老头子解解闷哦。
t_en: Ha! Just remember to come more often to chat with me.

t_ch: 我这儿虽然什么都缺，但可和你说好了，只要你来，<color=#dbc291ff>「卡利白」</color>就一定管够。
t_en: Although I don't have much of anything here, you and I have an agreement. Whenever you pop in, there will always be enough <color=#dbc291ff>Kapo-Kali</color> for you.

t_ch: 一致「宝藏」的发掘者：
t_en: To Treasure Hunters: (I)

t_ch: 呀呼！你被耍啦！
t_en: Ah hoo! You've been fooled!

t_ch: 其实，根本就没有什么「宝藏」。
t_en: Actually, there is no treasure at all.

t_ch: 唯一的大礼，就是藏在这房间里我的四张涂鸦手稿。我想你也认出来了，其中还有黑塔大人的风姿，没想到吧？
t_en: The only big gifts are my four graffiti drafts hidden in this room. And yes, there is also a nice picture of our respected Madam Herta. You didn't expect this, did you?

t_ch: 如果你是一名兢兢业业的小科员，深呼吸吧！千万别吓得倒在地板上死了。
t_en: If you are a devout little researcher, take a deep breath! Don't get a heart attack!

t_ch: 如果你是一个具有娱乐精神的年轻人，我建议你把它们都收集起来，生活不顺时就拿出来看看，在黯淡无光的塔内研究之余，给自己找点乐子。
t_en: If you are a fun-loving young person, I suggest you collect them all. Take them out when life is bad, and have some fun while studying in the dark tower.

t_ch: 呀哈哈的何塞•拉萨罗
t_en: Laughing Hosea Lazaro

t_ch: 二致「宝藏」的发掘者：
t_en: To Treasure Hunters: (II)

t_ch: 好吧，你能看到这段话，说明你听从了我的建议，具有百折不挠的宝贵品质。
t_en: Alright, if you can see this, it means you have followed my advice and that you possess the precious virtue of perseverance.

t_ch: 我承认，你绕了这么一大圈，我要是一根毛也没留下，是有点不地道。
t_en: I must admit, you've gone such a long way. If I don't give you something, it'll be a little mean.

t_ch: 毕竟我不知道过去这么多个琥珀纪后，来到这里的你到底是谁。
t_en: After all, I don't know who'd be looking for my treasure after so many Amber Eras.

t_ch: 有那么千分之一的可能，你是我何塞•拉萨罗在未来的卫道者、冉冉升起的精神接班人
t_en: There is a slim chance that maybe you are my future champion, my spiritual successor.

t_ch: 如果贸然误伤友军，岂不是很过意不去？
t_en: Wouldn't it be a pity if I accidentally hurt my friends?

t_ch: 没办法了，现在，我打算把真正的「宝藏」秀出来。
t_en: Seems like there's no other way. I have to show you the real treasure now.

t_ch: 你尊敬的何塞•拉萨罗
t_en: Your Respected Hosea Lazaro

t_ch: 三致「宝藏」的发掘者：
t_en: To Treasure Hunters: (III)

t_ch: 你喝过一种名叫「卡利白」的汽水吗？
t_en: Have you ever had a soda called Kapo-Kali?

t_ch: 它一度是空间站的招牌，风靡南垣星系的反重力饮品。
t_en: It was once the signature of the space station, an anti-gravity drink extremely popular in the Southern Orion system.

t_ch: 不好意思，除了大名鼎鼎的勘星钎与星域导影之外，毋庸置疑，那也是我何塞•拉萨罗的手笔。
t_en: Oh, sorry! Besides my other famous inventions, like the Astro Rod and Cosmos Guide, this is another one of my masterpieces.

t_ch: 「星际和平公司」曾经想花五百万信用点买下它的配方，被我拒绝了。
t_en: The Interastral Peace Corporation once wanted to spend five million credits to buy its recipe, and I refused.

t_ch: 「黑塔•万有应物科」的温槐仁曾经三十三次试图来偷师，被我打得鼻青脸肿。
t_en: Wen Huairen of the Herta Department of Implement Arts has tried to steal the secret from me <unbreak>33</unbreak> times — for which he was beaten to a pulp like he deserved.

t_ch: 现在，我将它的配方公之于下——
t_en: Now, I'll make it public once and for all.

t_ch: 它可值五百万啊。且听下回分解。
t_en: It's worth five million! Please stay tuned.

t_ch: 金牌调饮师何塞•拉萨罗
t_en: Legendary Drink-Maker Hosea Lazaro

t_ch: 四致「宝藏」的发掘者：
t_en: To Treasure Hunters: (IV)

t_ch: 不要怀疑自己的眼睛，以上就是「卡利白」的珍贵秘方。
t_en: Do not doubt your eyes. Above is the precious secret recipe of Kapo-Kali.

t_ch: 诓你的人是小狗。
t_en: I swear on my mother's grave.

t_ch: 如果你是自己人，你会知道上哪儿破译上方这一串密码。
t_en: If you are one of us, you will know where to decode the password above.

t_ch: 提示：它需要用到一种特殊显影法。
t_en: Tip: A special method is required to reveal the text.

t_ch: 绝对够意思的何塞•拉萨罗
t_en: Hosea Lazaro the Terrific

t_ch: 这里应该就是藏宝图标示的房间所在了。
t_en: This should be the room shown on the treasure map.

t_ch: 经由特殊显影法处理的藏宝图呈现出黑白的暗色调，唯独用红圈标示出了目标房间位置。
t_en: The treasure map revealed by a special autoradiograph is in black and white. Only the location of the target room is marked with a red circle.

t_ch: （…莫名其妙。不过，艾丝妲和阿兰似乎摊上麻烦了……）
t_en: (...So strange. Seems like Asta and Arlan are in trouble...)

t_ch: （这种捕风捉影的事情，还是不要让当事人知道比较好。）
t_en: (Still, it all sounds like hearsay to me. Better keep them in the dark for now.)

t_ch: （姬子跟空间站的人好像很熟，还是先找她商量商量吧。）
t_en: (Himeko seems to be on good terms with the folks on the space station. Maybe I should tell her first.)

t_ch: 出什么事了吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Is something the matter, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: （说出群聊爆料的事。）
t_en: (Recount the situation in the group chat to Himeko.)

t_ch: 艾丝妲这个站长当得也真不容易。
t_en: It must be difficult for Asta. Being the lead researcher isn't easy.

t_ch: 前脚「军团」刚走，后脚就有人造谣生事…那句话怎么说来着？「对外敌我唯唯诺诺，搞内耗我重拳出击」……
t_en: As soon as the Legion exits stage right, in comes the gossip... How does that saying go again? "Greet enemies outside with a smile, greet enemies inside with a sword..."

t_ch: 黑塔的企业文化嘛……
t_en: Such is the Herta company culture...

t_ch: 你看起来还挺开心的。
t_en: You seem pretty cheerful about it.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你想插手这件事吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you thinking of getting involved?

t_ch: 其实我也只想看看乐子…
t_en: No, just eating popcorn on the sidelines...

t_ch: 我还挺想帮帮艾丝妲的。
t_en: I really want to help Asta.

t_ch: 那怎么能呢？毕竟是这么有…麻烦的状况。
t_en: What do you mean? This is such an inter— Uh... inconvenient situation.

t_ch: 有其企业家必有其文化。
t_en: Company culture stems from the company owner...

t_ch: 哈哈，黑塔才不管这个地方呢。不如说有人的地方，就会产生江湖吧。
t_en: Haha, Herta doesn't really have anything to do with it. I'm afraid wherever you find people, you find gossip of some variety.

t_ch: 嗯，其实这类谣言本与我们无关，但事关艾丝妲那姑娘的声誉，佯装不知也不是无名客的作风。
t_en: Well, while these rumors have nothing to do with us, they do concern the reputation of young Asta. And turning a blind eye is not the way of the Nameless.

t_ch: 处理这种事情并不困难。群众关心的是戏剧性而并非事实，不用揭开真相，只要阻止谣言发酵就好了。比起辛辛苦苦地辟谣，不如直接找出谣言的制造者。
t_en: Solving a matter like this won't be difficult. Predictably, the crowd cares more about the drama than the facts. Stopping the rumors from brewing is more important right now than laying out the truth... and it's better to unearth the source of the rumors directly than to painstakingly try to refute them one by one.

t_ch: 一切唯姬子马首是瞻。
t_en: As always, your guidance is wise.

t_ch: 你尽会说好听的，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're a smooth talker, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 解决出问题的人是吧？
t_en: So we get rid of the troublemaker, right?

t_ch: 不不，那可不是一回事……
t_en: No, no... that's not what I meant...

t_ch: 我有个朋友，据说这空间站里就没有她不知道的八卦消息。
t_en: I have a friend — they say there isn't a single piece of station gossip she doesn't know about.

t_ch: 这个朋友是不是你自己？
t_en: Talking about yourself?

t_ch: 看不出来姬子你也爱好八卦…
t_en: I didn't realize you liked gossip too, Himeko...

t_ch: 首先，我的朋友不是我；其次，我交朋友也不是为了听八卦呀。
t_en: First of all, it's not me. Secondly, I don't make friends JUST for the sake of gossip.

t_ch: 只是我这个朋友神通广大，我想你也许能从她那边套出什么情报。这个时间…我看看，她一定是在科室外面翘班摸鱼。
t_en: It's just that my friend is pretty amazing, and I think you might be able to extract some information from her. Let's see... Right now she's probably slacking off somewhere outside the department.

t_ch: 我不知道她的本名，只知道她自称——<color=#dbc291ff>「瓜农」</color>。
t_en: I don't know her real name, but she calls herself <color=#dbc291ff>Gossipmonger</color>....

t_ch: （好像没有人在偷懒…难道「瓜农」不在这里？）
t_en: (I can't see anyone slacking off... Maybe Gossipmonger isn't around?)

t_ch: 是阿兰大哥托你来调查的吗？太好了，正巧我有些新发现。
t_en: Did Arlan ask you to investigate? Great! I've made some new discoveries.

t_ch: （流言已经开始发酵了，先在周围收集一下情报吧。）
t_en: (The rumors are already circulating. Let's gather some intel first.)

t_ch: 怎么说，「瓜农」有什么劲爆内幕吗？站长真的有贪污？那个举报人是谁啊这么狂？
t_en: Have you heard anything from Gossipmonger? Is the lead researcher really that corrupt? Who exactly is this whistleblower?

t_ch: 咦，你今天不是轮休么？
t_en: Hey, aren't you off work today?

t_ch: 刷了下手机这不就赶过来了嘛，轮休哪有吃瓜重要！
t_en: I couldn't not come in after checking my phone! Days off aren't as juicy as gossip.

t_ch: 可惜我一早上也没碰上「瓜农」，不如你回去躺着等消息？
t_en: Well, too bad, I haven't seen Gossipmonger this morning. You should rest up and wait for more news.

t_ch: 说得也是……
t_en: *sigh* You're right...

t_ch: 你怎么还在这儿？你们科室没喊你去准备急救手术吗？
t_en: Why are you still here? Didn't your department call you to prepare for the emergency operation?

t_ch: 好像…没有？我才偷偷溜出来转了会儿，没这么巧吧……
t_en: Uh... no? I just sneaked out for a little while. What are the odds...

t_ch: 2分钟前我收到群发消息，说<color=#dbc291ff>特殊解析室那边出事了，有人被困住了</color>，现在防卫科把入口的区域都封起来了。
t_en: I got a group message two minutes ago saying that <color=#dbc291ff>something's happened in the Special Purpose Lab — somebody's trapped</color>. The Security Department's blocked off the area around the entrance.

t_ch: 特殊解析室？那<color=#dbc291ff>「瓜农」帕梅拉岂不是也……</color>
t_en: The Special Purpose Lab? Then wouldn't <color=#dbc291ff>Pamela — I mean, Gossipmonger be...</color>

t_ch: 多半是了，难怪这个时候没见着她来摸鱼……
t_en: Most likely. No wonder she's not here right now...

t_ch: 「瓜农」只关心八卦，却不关心八卦背后的秘密。
t_en: Gossipmonger only concerns herself with gossip, not the secrets within.

t_ch: 黑粉群人这么少，但消息却传得这么快，这背后必有推手……
t_en: There aren't many members in the Heritor group, but news is still traveling so fast. This must be the work of something bigger...

t_ch: …嗯…群聊的人数和内容…小号的自导自演？可这么做的目的到底是什么呢……
t_en: ...Hmm... Given the number of people in the group and its content... could this all stem from a few alt accounts? But why kick up a storm like this...

t_ch: 特殊解析室附近通路已经封锁，请无关人员不要靠近。
t_en: The walkways around the Special Purpose Lab have been temporarily closed off. Please keep your distance.

t_ch: 能不能通融下让我过去。
t_en: Can you let me through just this once?

t_ch: 那里有一些突发情况，现在很危险。
t_en: I'm afraid this is a live situation. It's too dangerous.

t_ch: 但…我听阿兰大哥说过你的事。你执意要去的话…我只能说，注意安全。
t_en: Wait... Brother Arlan told me about you. If you insist on going... then I wish you the best of luck. Be safe.

t_ch: 我只是路过的。
t_en: I'm just passing through.

t_ch: 你是…阿兰大哥的朋友！
t_en: You're... Brother Arlan's friend!

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 是这样，防卫科收到了「军团」残党在这附近的目击情报。
t_en: The Security Department received information about sightings of Legion remnants in the vicinity.

t_ch: 但就在刚刚，我们确认到这里还存在着前所未见的怪物。
t_en: However, we just confirmed that there are monsters here that have never been seen before.

t_ch: 虽然防卫科紧急封锁了这片区域，但已经有人失去了联系。
t_en: The Security Department sectioned off the area immediately, but we lost contact with some people.

t_ch: 帕梅拉在这里么？
t_en: Is Pamela here?

t_ch: 帕梅…拉？我看看，救援人员名单上确实有她的名字。
t_en: Pamela...? Let me check, I remember her name was on the rescuer list.

t_ch: 看到没有，这痕迹…空间站有「裂界」化迹象的传闻看来也不是毫无根据。
t_en: Did you see it? Look... It seems like the rumors about the station are true — there are Stellaron traces here.

t_ch: 是什么原因我不管。我只知道，怪物破坏了这里的电源设备。
t_en: Good luck figuring that out. All I know is that monsters have destroyed the power supply installations here.

t_ch: 现在电梯开不了，困在下面的人也上不来。
t_en: The elevator is out of commission, so the people stuck on the floor below can't make it up.

t_ch: 你们怎么不下去？
t_en: Why don't you go down there?

t_ch: 事发突然，防卫科的支援还在路上。
t_en: It happened so suddenly. The support from the Security Department is still on the way.

t_ch: 目前人手不太够，只能先守住入口不让怪物上来。
t_en: We're low on staff right now — all we can do is make sure the monsters don't make it up here.

t_ch: 我能做些什么？
t_en: Is there anything I can do?

t_ch: 或许，你可以协助我们重启中央电梯。
t_en: Maybe you can help us restart the central elevator?

t_ch: 收容舱段的备用电源有很多，没记错的话对面就有。
t_en: There are plenty of backup power sources in the storage zone — right opposite, if I remember right.

t_ch: 只要能恢复供电，就能重启电梯下去救人。
t_en: As soon as we can restart the power supply, we can get this elevator working and rescue everyone.

t_ch: 怎么样，备用电源还在吗？
t_en: Well, did you find the backup power source?

t_ch: （上交备用电源）
t_en: (Hand over the backup power source.)

t_ch: 嘶，还好有你在，这下够用了。稍等我去调试一下电梯。
t_en: Phew, thank goodness you're here. Give me a moment — I'll give the elevator a little shake-up.

t_ch: 好了，现在你可以下去了，注意安全。
t_en: Alright, you can use it now. Be safe.

t_ch: 救命啊，救命——等等，电梯来了！
t_en: Help! Somebody help! ...Wait, the elevator is here!

t_ch: 那个，帕梅拉她跟我们走丢了。那边有个很吓人的怪物，我们不敢过去。
t_en: W—We lost sight of Pamela... There's a terrifying monster over there — we didn't dare to go over on our own.

t_ch: 请你赶紧去救救她，我、我们自己坐电梯回去就好了。
t_en: Please save her! W—We'll take the elevator up.

t_ch: 拜托了！
t_en: Please!

t_ch: 妈耶，吓死姑奶奶我了…你、你怎么才来啊！
t_en: Holy mackerel, you scared the stars out of me... Y—You took your time!

t_ch: 你就是「瓜农」？
t_en: Are you Gossipmonger?

t_ch: 没错，我就是你要找的人。
t_en: That's right, I'm the one you're looking for.

t_ch: 你知道我会来找你？
t_en: You knew I was looking for you?

t_ch: 当然。我不仅知道你会来，我还知道你为什么而来。
t_en: Of course. Not only did I know you were coming, but I also know why.

t_ch: 我找你打听点事…
t_en: I came to ask you something...

t_ch: 呵，你不就是想知道黑粉群里的「瓜田里猹」到底是谁么？
t_en: Heh, you want to know who Richard_Knowitall from that Heritor group is, don't you?

t_ch: 黑粉？
t_en: Heritor?

t_ch: 黑塔女士的粉丝，简称黑粉。
t_en: Fans of Madam Herta. They call themselves the Heritors.

t_ch: 我是个生意人，生意人有生意人的规矩。你想知道消息，就得付出代价。
t_en: I'm a businesswoman, and we play by our own rules. If you want the scoop, you have to pay the price.

t_ch: 我救了你，这还不够？
t_en: I saved you, isn't that enough?

t_ch: 那必须不能够啊。我这才值多少点，人总得有个自知之明不是？
t_en: Certainly not. I have a very clear notion of how much different favors cost.

t_ch: 那么，代价是什么呢？
t_en: Well then, what's the price?

t_ch: 放心，生意人讲的就是个公道，我又怎么会为难你？
t_en: Don't worry, the price is a fair one. Why would I make it difficult for you?

t_ch: 我这人吧，平时也没啥爱好，就爱听听八卦，然后…崇拜崇拜…这个，科学家。
t_en: I don't have any particular hobbies. I do indulge in a little tittle-tattling here and there, not to mention, admiring... of a certain scientist...

t_ch: 那什么，我听说你跟星穹列车那伙人是一起打过小怪兽的关系？
t_en: A—And now here you are, vanquishing monsters left, right, and center! I heard you're with the Astral Express...?

t_ch: 巧了不是，在下就喜欢姬子姐姐这种美貌与智慧并兼，优雅又不失温和的…科学家。
t_en: How fortunate! As it happens, I'm a huge fan of Himeko... An amalgamation of beauty and brains — the epitome of a graceful and gentle... scientist.

t_ch: 所以？
t_en: Soooo...?

t_ch: 所以你能不能帮人家弄到姬子姐姐的高清无死角正脸笑容照嘛～
t_en: Can you help me take a hi-def portrait photo of Himeko smiling? Pleeeaase~

t_ch: 要眼里能放出十万伏特的微笑电力，能把人家都电晕的那种笑容哦～
t_en: Make sure you capture her electrifying smile — the one that stuns people into a trance — that million-volt smile~

t_ch: 没听过这么奇怪的要求。
t_en: This is a strange request.

t_ch: 干嘛啦～你和姬子姐姐很熟的吧？你去求她，她肯定会愿意的啦～
t_en: What's the matter? You know Himeko well, right? If you ask her, I'm sure she'll say yes~

t_ch: 好吧，试试就逝世！
t_en: Alright, though it'll probably be my funeral...

t_ch: 我就欣赏你这种年少有为的勇士！
t_en: I appreciate young, brave, talented people like yourself!

t_ch: 一张超绝美丽电光四射的姬子姐姐大头照换「瓜田里猹」的小秘密。
t_en: A superbly beautiful, dazzling, electrifying portrait shot of Himeko in exchange for the secret behind Richard_Knowitall.

t_ch: 照片到手，你直接发消息给我就好。一物换一物，我做生意一向公平。
t_en: Send the picture over when you have it. I'm always fair when it comes to haggling.

t_ch: 你怎么知道？
t_en: How did you know?

t_ch: 我要是连这都不知道，岂不枉称个「瓜农」虚名？
t_en: If I didn't even know that, could I really call myself Gossipmonger?

t_ch: 你别这样，正常一点…
t_en: Do you have a request that's more... normal?

t_ch: 找到「瓜农」了吗？她怎么说。
t_en: Did you find Gossipmonger? What did she say?

t_ch: 她说拿你的照片来换。
t_en: She wants your picture in exchange for information.

t_ch: 哈，我就觉得她怪怪的……
t_en: Ha, I knew she was weird...

t_ch: 你说你们是朋友。
t_en: You said you were friends.

t_ch: 唉，朋友也分很多种…这种呢，就是不照面的时候才能当朋友……
t_en: *sigh* There are many types of friend... She's the type you can only stay friends with if you never meet her.

t_ch: 算啦，一张照片而已，我是不介意啦。{NICKNAME}，有需要的话你就拍呗。
t_en: It's okay. It's just a picture. I don't mind. Go ahead, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 给我看看…咦，拍照有一手嘛，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let me see... Hey, you're pretty good, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 什么什么，在拍什么？
t_en: Huh? What are you taking pictures of?

t_ch: 你、你怎么来了？
t_en: Wh—What brings you here?

t_ch: 一定是三月七传染的。
t_en: March 7th may have rubbed off on me.

t_ch: 看到我这么慌张干嘛？…好可疑。
t_en: Why do you sound so nervous all of a sudden? Suspicious...

t_ch: 没想到你会来吧，不是说你最近很忙吗？
t_en: I didn't expect to see you here. Didn't you say you were really busy?

t_ch: 唉，是啊。「军团」刚刚退走，空间站又是这个百废待兴的状态。
t_en: *sigh* I am. The Legion has only just retreated — the station is in absolute shambles.

t_ch: 重建工作才小有进展，最近局部又出现了裂界化的迹象……
t_en: And now any progress in rebuilding things has been ruined by the reappearance of the Fragmentum...

t_ch: 星核被激活了，该来的总是会来…裂界化的事，黑塔怎么说？
t_en: Oh, an active Stellaron is rarely a cause for celebration... What did Herta say about the Fragmentum's appearance?

t_ch: 老师的性子您知道的，就跟无事发生一样，又当甩手掌柜了。
t_en: You know what she's like. It's like nothing's happened — she's just sitting back and giving orders.

t_ch: 后面有空，我还得再找您商量商量。毕竟事关裂界，你们才是专家。
t_en: If there's time later, I want to talk to you both in more detail about this. You're the Fragmentum experts after all.

t_ch: 随时候教。
t_en: Anytime.

t_ch: 唉，我先走了。家里给我支取零花钱的密钥被人给扣了，我得赶紧去处理下。
t_en: *sigh* I need to go — someone's taken my family encryption key. You know, the one I use to withdraw my allowance? I need to get to the bottom of it.

t_ch: 被人扣住的密钥…
t_en: Someone's withholding her encryption key...

t_ch: 谣言制造者说他掌握的证据也许就是这个，{NICKNAME}，看来你得抓紧时间了。
t_en: That might be the whistleblower's so-called "evidence." Looks like you don't have much time to lose, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>戴黑框眼镜的斜刘海</color>小伙，让我看看哪个才是「瓜田里猹」？）
t_en: (A young man <color=#dbc291ff>with black-rimmed glasses and a slanted fringe</color>. Which one is Richard_Knowitall?)

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 你是？
t_en: And you are?

t_ch: 你就是「瓜田里猹」？
t_en: Are you Richard_Knowitall?

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 什么寡头理查…你在说什么啊。
t_en: Richard knows what now...? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 就是你小子造谣艾丝妲？
t_en: Are you the one who started the rumor about Asta?

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 你别乱说啊，我什么时候造谣过站长，莫名其妙。
t_en: What are you talking about? When did I start rumors about the lead researcher? What nonsense.

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 我不认识你，你再这样我可要喊人了。
t_en: I don't even know you. I'm gonna call security if you keep this up.

t_ch: 你是？
t_en: And you are?

t_ch: 你就是「瓜田里猹」？
t_en: Are you Richard_Knowitall?

t_ch: 哟呵，行啊，这都被你发现了。
t_en: Oho! Well done — you found me.

t_ch: 就是你小子造谣艾丝妲？
t_en: Are you the one who started the rumor about Asta?

t_ch: 哈哈，这叫「造谣」？这叫公开检举！
t_en: Rumors? More like a formal complaint!

t_ch: 你是为了站长中饱私囊的事来的？没想到你这么八卦…居然还追了过来。
t_en: So you're here about the lead researcher's corruption? Anything for a good story, huh?

t_ch: 我真搞不懂你们这些人，看点八卦而已，怎么就憋着这么一大股劲儿。
t_en: I don't get it — you people live your lives for gossip.

t_ch: 拿这股劲头去搞研究该多好，黑塔女士看到大家这么努力工作一定会很开心的。
t_en: Wouldn't it be better to apply that drive to your research? Madam Herta would be delighted to see everyone working so hard.

t_ch: 我是来制裁你的。
t_en: I'm here to punish you.

t_ch: 哈哈，你谁啊，你以为你是下一任空间站站长啊？
t_en: Haha, who do you think you are? The space station's next lead researcher?

t_ch: 拜托，我这种公正不阿、聪明好学的正直之人当站长还差不多，你一个听乐子的，搞清楚自己的定位好吧。
t_en: Please. Only upstanding and intelligent people like myself are worthy of such a position. You gossipers need to figure out your place in the hierarchy.

t_ch: 把艾丝妲的密钥交出来。
t_en: Hand over Asta's encryption key.

t_ch: 什么密钥……
t_en: What encryption key...

t_ch: 你是不是看了什么老掉牙的小说啊。还以为空间站里有什么隐世家族霸道总裁，动不动就是金库密钥的…少幻想多学习，好不好？
t_en: Have you been reading too many novels? You think there's some hidden heir on this space station with keys to the family coffers? Less fantasizing, more researching, got it?

t_ch: 算了，你这么想要「猛料」…哼哼，反正我都备份了，给你看看，也知道我瓜田里猹不是造谣生事的人。
t_en: You want the scoop? Fine. I've backed it all up anyway. Here, take it. And let it be known that I, Richard_Knowitall, do not spread rumors.

t_ch: 就是这张单据，你看看那个采购合同上的抬头——
t_en: This is the receipt. See the title on this purchase order...?

t_ch: 这就是我说的，重量级的实质性证据！哼哼……
t_en: That's what I call substantive evidence! Hmph...

t_ch: 文件来源可靠吗？
t_en: Is the source of this document reliable?

t_ch: 那当然！我敢爆料，难道还造假不成？
t_en: Of course it is! If I had any doubt, I'd have kept it quiet.

t_ch: 这玩意嘛…就是前几天我去取包裹的时候，看到前台有几张单子指名站长签收。
t_en: ...I picked it up a few days ago when I was collecting a package. I saw a few receipts requiring the lead researcher's signature.

t_ch: 防卫科的一把手阿兰看了眼单子就急匆匆地走了，谁不知道他是艾丝妲的亲信。
t_en: The Security Department's head honcho, Arlan — he left pretty quickly when he saw them. We all know he's on her side anyway.

t_ch: 我凑上去一看，哟呵，好家伙——用私人名义购买空间站用的仪器，可被我逮到了！
t_en: So I had a peek, and woah! She's buying equipment for the space station under her own name! I've got her now!

t_ch: 这算哪门子证据啊……
t_en: This is no evidence at all...

t_ch: 啧，非得我把证据掰碎了喂你是吧？
t_en: Geez, do I really have to break it down for you?

t_ch: 空间站有财务流程。咱们无论采购什么东西，只要是给空间站买的，都得走对公账户，用公司批的预算。
t_en: The space station has its own financial procedures. As long as the purchase is for the space station, no matter how big or small, it has to go through the organizational account using the station's allocated funds.

t_ch: 她这个付款方写私人账号，显然就是公款私用啊！
t_en: The payer here is her private account. She's totally embezzling station funds for herself!

t_ch: 失望呐，痛心呐。看起来挺干净一小姑娘，得到黑塔大人的提拔不报恩就算了，还搞这些个腌臜事儿。
t_en: I'm so disappointed — so hurt! She looks like a sweet enough girl, but not only did she fail to repay Madam Herta for promoting her, she even stooped to this.

t_ch: 我当时真就被气到浑身发抖，手脚冰凉，大热天的全身冒冷汗。
t_en: I was so angry that I was shaking. I broke out in a cold sweat!

t_ch: 会不会是她自己买的？
t_en: Have you ever considered that... maybe she paid for it herself?

t_ch: 这么大的金额，她自己掏钱？什么家庭啊，弗莱明亲女儿啊？
t_en: That amount of money? Herself!? Who do you think she is, Fleming's daughter?

t_ch: 好了，猛料你也看了，别烦我了。我还要写举报信给黑塔女士呢，忙得很！
t_en: Anyway, now you know everything, so leave me alone. I still need to write a report about this for Madam Herta.

t_ch: （看来只是虚惊一场……）
t_en: (Seems like it's all just a scare...)

t_ch: （不过造谣一张嘴，辟谣跑断腿。还是去提醒艾丝妲一下吧。）
t_en: (But it's easy to make rumors and hard to dispel them. I better let Asta know.)

t_ch: 呃，你好，有什么事吗？
t_en: Uh, hi, how can I help you?

t_ch: 你就是「瓜田里猹」？
t_en: Are you Richard_Knowitall?

t_ch: 噢你是说在群里爆料的那个？
t_en: Oh, you mean the guy from the group chat — the one with the scoop?

t_ch: 这件事我也有所耳闻。瓜嘛，甜就好，不要当真……
t_en: I found out about the whole thing recently — drama for drama's sake, y'know? Don't take it too seriously...

t_ch: 就是你小子造谣艾丝妲？
t_en: Are you the one who started the rumor about Asta?

t_ch: 造谣？什么意思？…你是不是找错人了啊。
t_en: Started it? What do you mean? I think you have the wrong person.

t_ch: 我这两天一直在加班，都没离开过科室……
t_en: I've been on overtime for the past two days — I haven't left my office...

t_ch: 哎，看来我加班期间发生了很多事啊……
t_en: *sigh* Sounds like a lot's happened since I last poked my head out the door...

t_ch: （好像和「瓜农」说的样子对不上号啊……）
t_en: (Doesn't fit Gossipmonger's descriptions...)

t_ch: （她说的…好像是个<color=#dbc291ff>戴黑框眼镜的斜刘海</color>小伙吧。）
t_en: (She said... a young man with <color=#dbc291ff>black-rimmed glasses and a slanted fringe</color>.)

t_ch: 她对你图谋不轨没安好心。
t_en: She's made a very Himeko-centric proposal.

t_ch: 不能答应她啊！
t_en: You can't give in to her!

t_ch: 美颜开几级？要滤镜吗？
t_en: What level of beauty filter do you want for the photo?

t_ch: 你这样乱花钱可不行……
t_en: You can't waste money like this...

t_ch: 这叫什么乱花钱？公司的预算卡那么死，流程又那么长，等我申请完下来那要浪费多少时间？！
t_en: This isn't wasting money! The IPC's budget is so stringent, and the process takes so long! By the time my application would've gone through too much time would've been wasted!

t_ch: 「军团」的袭击毁了多少硬件，现在站里瘫痪了多少管线你知道么？
t_en: Do you know how much hardware was destroyed when the Legion attacked? And how many cables are currently out of order on the station?

t_ch: 我自掏腰包给大家解决问题还是我的错了？你赶紧把我的密钥还过来！
t_en: How is it my fault when I'm spending my own money to solve everyone else's problems? Give my encryption key back!

t_ch: 我不！
t_en: I won't!

t_ch: 你！谁给你的权利管起我的吃穿用度来了？
t_en: You — who gave you the right to tell me what to do with my money?

t_ch: 你。
t_en: You did.

t_ch: 我…呃，好像确实有这么回事，可、可这不算乱花钱啊。
t_en: I... I might have done that, but this doesn't count as wasting money!

t_ch: 这怎么不算？之前买的那个叫「查宝」的寻物仪，碰上军团入侵丢件了。
t_en: How is it not wasting money? We lost that Findie device you bought during the Legion's invasion.

t_ch: 明明查一下库存就能补送的东西，你非要急着重新下单。
t_en: All you had to do was check the inventory, and we could've replaced it — why did you make a new order?

t_ch: 还有上次那个歼星舰……
t_en: And that starnought fleet from the last time...

t_ch: 歼、歼星舰？！
t_en: S—Starnought!?

t_ch: 咳，别误会。这个，虽然下了单，但买不到啊…我这不也是为了加强安防保证这次的袭击事件不再出现嘛……
t_en: I did make an order, but it didn't go through... I was just trying to improve security measures to make sure attacks like this don't happen again.

t_ch: 没、没乱花出去的就不算乱花！…{NICKNAME}，你有事找我？
t_en: I—If the money wasn't spent, then it doesn't count! Anyway, {NICKNAME}, are you here for something?

t_ch: 我觉得没刚刚的事重要。
t_en: Nothing as important as what you were just discussing.

t_ch: 你这话题转得有点僵硬。
t_en: That's a very abrupt way to change the topic.

t_ch: 你好烦啊，说正事儿。
t_en: Ugh, please, tell me why you're here.

t_ch: （告知理查的所作所为）
t_en: (Tell her about Richard's actions)

t_ch: 所以，你以为是理查偷走了我的密钥？
t_en: So, you think Richard was the one who stole my encryption key?

t_ch: 不过原来是他在造谣…嗯，倒也符合他人设。
t_en: So he was the one who started the rumors... Hmm, that fits.

t_ch: （感觉她好像…并不意外？）
t_en: (It feels like she's... not surprised?)

t_ch: 这件事我马上就去处理。请不用担心，大小姐。
t_en: I'll deal with that immediately. Please, don't worry, Miss Asta.

t_ch: 去吧去吧，哎等等，密钥给我留下。
t_en: Go, go... Wait! Leave the encryption key here.

t_ch: 一码归一码，密钥的事容后再议。
t_en: That's a separate issue. We'll talk about the encryption key later.

t_ch: 欸，怎么能这样——
t_en: Huh? How can you...

t_ch: 三重权限认证缺失，禁止开启舱门。
t_en: Triple authentication failed. Opening the hatch is forbidden.

t_ch: （就连「黑塔」特许通行认证也无法开启舱门——此处需要所谓三重权限认证。）
t_en: (Even the Space Station Special Permit is unable to open this hatch — a so-called triple authentication is required here.)

t_ch: （看来要想进入这个神秘的舱室，还得多费一番功夫。）
t_en: (Entering this mysterious cabin seems to require some extra effort.)

t_ch: 在嘘实埋间的僻静终点，寂寞蒙尘的地面上，发现了黯然闪烁的卡片。
t_en: On the dusty floor of the room at a quiet corner of the Disposal Site, a card can be seen glimmering faintly.

t_ch: 隐约可以辨认出是一张特殊权限卡——残角破碎，字面磨损，不知是否还可投入使用。
t_en: This special access card is vaguely distinguishable from the others — the corners are chipped and the writings are worn out. It's unclear if it works.

t_ch: 随着偶然的光线折射，可以发现上面印着模糊的「千星」字样。
t_en: When the light is at a certain angle, the words "Thousand Stars" can vaguely be seen printed on the card.

t_ch: 截至目前，你已经获取了三张来历不明的权限认证卡。
t_en: So far, you have obtained three authentication cards of unknown origins.

t_ch: 在「波少」群情激昂的粉丝脚下，发现了奇怪的卡片。它狡黠闪烁，像在嘲讽这场闹剧。
t_en: Under the feet of Master Capote's passionate fans, a strange card was found. It flickered cunningly, as if mocking the farce.

t_ch: 隐约可以辨认出是一张特殊权限卡——残角破碎，字面磨损，不知是否还可投入使用。
t_en: This special access card is vaguely distinguishable from the others — the corners are chipped and the writings are worn out. It's unclear if it works.

t_ch: 随着偶然的光线折射，可以发现上面印着完全磨损的「██」字样。
t_en: When the light is at a certain angle, the words "██" can vaguely be seen printed on the card.

t_ch: 将三张权限认证卡重合，置于背光条件下，发现它以这种隐秘的方式印刻着「千星██集团」字样。
t_en: By stacking the three authority authentication cards on top of each other and placing them against the light, the words "Thousands Stars ██ Corporation" can vaguely be seen printed on the cards.

t_ch: （是时候了，前往开启三重权限所守卫的「那扇门」。）
t_en: (It's time to go and open the door protected by triple authentication.)

t_ch: （想必这就是三重权限所守卫的「那扇门」。）
t_en: (This must be the door protected by triple authentication.)

t_ch: （动手吧。）
t_en: (Let's do it.)

t_ch: 是否进行三重权限认证，开启舱门？
t_en: Proceed with triple authentication to open the hatch?

t_ch: 一重…二重…三重权限认证通过。
t_en: First... Second... Third authentication passed.

t_ch: 距离您上次来到这里，已经过去了数个黑塔系统年。
t_en: It has been several space station system years since you last came.

t_ch: 董事会一直竭诚等待着您的回复。无论如何，欢迎回来——
t_en: The board of directors has been waiting eagerly for your reply. Anyway, welcome back—

t_ch: 艾丝妲小姐。
t_en: Miss Asta.

t_ch: （「艾丝妲小姐」？）
t_en: (Miss Asta?)

t_ch: （难怪这里关卡重重，原来是她弃置不用的工作房间——称之为站长套房也不为过。）
t_en: (No wonder there are so many security measures here. It turns out this was once her office — you can even call this a lead researcher's suite.)

t_ch: 正在启动权限认证…认证中…认证失败！
t_en: Initiating authentication verification... Verifying... Verification failed!

t_ch: 未识别到权限认证卡，请确保已携带权限认证卡后再启动。
t_en: Authentication verification card not detected. Please re-initiate after making sure you have the authentication verification card.

t_ch: 温馨提示：在启动认证程序前，请确保携带完整的<color=#dbc291ff>三重</color>权限认证卡。
t_en: Reminder: Please make sure you have the complete <color=#dbc291ff>triple</color> authentication verification card before you launch the verification program.

t_ch: 如需帮助，请选择：
t_en: If you need help, please select:

t_ch: 权限认证卡遗失找回。
t_en: Retrieve authentication verification card.

t_ch: 正在提交终端访问权限申请，请耐心等待…申请中…申请中…访问通过。
t_en: Submitting request to access the terminal. Please wait... Submitting... Submitting... Access granted.

t_ch: 请选择你要查询的认证序列：
t_en: Please select the certification sequence you wish to access:

t_ch: 第一重权限。
t_en: Tier-1 certification.

t_ch: 同步程序激活中…激活成功！确认坐标信息来源于<color=#dbc291ff>主控舱段</color>。
t_en: Activating synchronization program... Activation successful! Beacon signal is coming from the <color=#dbc291ff>master control zone</color>.

t_ch: 正在同步监控信息…解析中…请稍后——
t_en: Currently synchronizing to surveillance data... Analyzing... Please wait—

t_ch: 你看见一段模糊不清的监控录像，最后的画面停留在一个<color=#dbc291ff>爆炸头发型</color>的科员蹲下，将手伸向尽头的画面。
t_en: You see a blurry surveillance video recording. The final image is of a researcher <color=#dbc291ff>with a luscious hairstyle</color> kneeling down and reaching toward something.

t_ch: 第二重权限。
t_en: Tier-2 certification.

t_ch: 同步程序激活中…激活成功！确认坐标信息来源于<color=#dbc291ff>收容舱段</color>。
t_en: Activating synchronization program... Activation successful! Beacon signal is coming from the <color=#dbc291ff>storage zone</color>.

t_ch: 正在同步监控信息…解析中…请稍后——
t_en: Currently synchronizing to surveillance data... Analyzing... Please wait—

t_ch: 提取到的监控录像极为模糊，只能依稀看见几只<color=#dbc291ff>呜呜伯</color>在落地窗外漂浮向下的画面，这似乎是某个<color=#dbc291ff>高处的舱室</color>。
t_en: The surveillance video you could access is extremely blurry. You can only see some <color=#dbc291ff>Wubbaboos</color> floating downward outside a wall-sized window. This seems to be an <color=#dbc291ff>elevated room somewhere</color>.

t_ch: 第三重权限。
t_en: Tier-3 certification.

t_ch: 同步程序激活中…激活成功！确认坐标信息来源于<color=#dbc291ff>基座舱段</color>，坐标信号强，信号来源距离小于三百米！
t_en: Activating synchronization program... Activation successful! Beacon signal is coming from the <color=#dbc291ff>base zone</color>. The signal is very strong, and is within <unbreak>300</unbreak> meters!

t_ch: 正在解析信号画面…解析中…解析成功！
t_en: Analyzing incoming visuals... Analyzing... Analysis successful!

t_ch: 你看到一段视角古怪的画面，在仔细辨认后，你确信画面中的身躯正是反物质军团的残党，镜头似乎挂在这个怪物的身上。
t_en: You see a group of images shot in strange angles. After much thought, you become convinced that the torso in the images is a remnant of the Antimatter Legion. The camera seems to be attached on this monster's body.

t_ch: 暂时不需要了。
t_en: Not now.

t_ch: 退出。
t_en: Exit.

t_ch: 哼，暮气沉沉，千篇一律……
t_en: Hum! Nothing special, so boring here...

t_ch: 所有人都穿着相同制式的衣服，简直是对科员个性的抹杀！
t_en: Everyone is wearing the same clothes. This is an obliteration of the staff's individuality!

t_ch: 这发型真酷！
t_en: This hairstyle is so cool!

t_ch: 嗯，不错，很有眼光。空间站框定了科员的着装规范，我不得已正在用细节表达我的态度。
t_en: Mm... not bad, you've got nice taste. The space station has a strict dress code for all researchers. I have to express myself with the smaller details.

t_ch: 挑染？太隐晦。妹妹头？太费眼睛。光头，太扎手。这个发型正好，又大又卷又膨，甩起来还带劲。
t_en: Highlights? Too obscure. Bob cut? Too unsightly. Bald? Too hard to pull off. Now this hairstyle is just right. It's big, curly and puffy. It feels so good when I swing my head.

t_ch: 怎么有个非主流…
t_en: Well... Didn't expect to be running into the fashion police today...

t_ch: 非主流？我的确独立于「主流」之外！这么说来…承蒙谬赞！
t_en: Fashion police? It's true, I do have quite the authority on fashion! So... Thanks!

t_ch: 你这白毛倒是不错，看来和我一样对于「潮流发型」颇具心得。
t_en: Your silver hair is not bad either. It seems you and I have similar tastes in hairstyle.

t_ch: 上一个能够跟我产生灵魂共振的，还是我母星的一位「编剧」——所谓编剧，也就是银河中的说书人。
t_en: The last person I connected with was a "screenwriter" on my home planet — we call them screenwriters. In the cosmos, you call them storytellers.

t_ch: 这头在哪儿做的？
t_en: Where did you get your hair done?

t_ch: 只要有心，哪里不是天地？你只需要到收容舱段取上一根「黯柴」——不过听说这等奇物全被「反物质军团」偷光了——再点燃实验台上的「无明磷粉」……
t_en: As long as you are determined, anything is possible, anywhere! All you have to do is pick up some Dark Matches from the storage zone — Oh, although I heard all these things have been stolen by the Antimatter Legion — anyway, then you light up a bit of Lightless Phosphorous Powder on the experiment bench...

t_ch: 心中默念，「巴卜昂乓，心想事成」……
t_en: Recite to yourself, "Babracadabra, make my wish plethora," then...

t_ch: 嘭！你就能得到一个蓬松度精确到发梢的爆炸头。
t_en: Boom! There you have it! Your very own luscious hairstyle.

t_ch: 好了好了，按理来说，我不该和「无敌宇宙核弹头协会」的编外人员说话。但这毕竟是我们<color=#dbc291ff>第一次</color>谈话……
t_en: Okay, okay. Normally I am not supposed to talk to people outside of the Invincible Space Warhead Association. But after all, this is our <color=#dbc291ff>first</color> conversation...

t_ch: 以后，要是脑电波对不上，就不必浪费彼此时间了。
t_en: In the future, if we can't get what the other is talking about, then let's not waste each other's time.

t_ch: 嘘…你果然来找我<color=#dbc291ff>第二次</color>啦。「无敌宇宙核弹头协会」，有没有兴趣了解一下？
t_en: Hush... So you did come back for a <color=#dbc291ff>second</color> conversation with me. Are you interested in learning about the Invincible Space Warhead Association?

t_ch: 听起来好厉害的样子！
t_en: That sounds awesome!

t_ch: 嗯～上道！
t_en: Yeah, you are on the right track!

t_ch: 不就是爆炸头协会吗…
t_en: It's just the Curlcademy, right?

t_ch: 去去去，你懂什么？！重要的是要有一个酷炫的名头。
t_en: Hey! What do you know!? It's important for an organization to have a cool name.

t_ch: 照你这么说，那寰宇趋之若鹜的「星核」，不就是——算了，这个不能说。
t_en: According to your theory, the "Stellaron" everyone is scrambling for is just a — oh, forget it. I can't tell you this.

t_ch: 目前「无敌宇宙核弹头协会」虽处于初创时期，但人员扩招势头是大大的向好。
t_en: Right now, although the Invincible Space Warhead Association is still in its initial stage, we are having great momentum when it comes to expanding our numbers.

t_ch: 在空间站隐秘的犄角旮旯，你不经意间就能看到又大又卷又膨的自己人……
t_en: Before long, we will have big, curly, puffy members in every hidden corner of the space station.

t_ch: 更别说我们已经拿到了「星际和平公司」的风投，研究「无明磷粉」与发量之间的黄金配比……
t_en: Not to mention, we have secured investment capital from the Interastral Peace Corporation to study the correlation between Lightless Phosphorous Powder and hair volume.

t_ch: 现在正是上车的好机会！等我们发展成「无敌宇宙核弹头公司」，可就没外人什么事了。
t_en: Now is a good chance to join us! When we develop into the Invincible Space Warhead Corporation, there will be no room for outsiders.

t_ch: 加入的方式也很简单，先变得又大又卷又膨，再来找我登记即可。
t_en: The way to join us is also very simple. First become big, curly and puffy, then you can sign up with me.

t_ch: 这样好的感觉，跟我打过<color=#dbc291ff>三次</color>交道的你仍然没有心动吗？
t_en: With such a great feeling, are you still not impressed after having dealt with me <color=#dbc291ff>three</color> times?

t_ch: 啊…今天也是又大又卷又膨的愉快一天。
t_en: Oh... Today is another big, curly, and puffy day.

t_ch: 你不用上班吗？
t_en: Don't you have to work?

t_ch: 在我默念「巴卜昂乓，心想事成」之后，我的实验工位就被炸毁了。
t_en: My experiment station got blown up after I recited "Babracadabra, make my wish plethora."

t_ch: 在防卫科替我安排新的工位之前，我的研究工作都处于暂停状态。
t_en: My research is suspended until the Security Department arranges a new station for me.

t_ch: 你是哪个科室的？
t_en: Which department are you from?

t_ch: 不知道，不重要。总有一天，空间站会增设一门「黑塔•狂野爆炸科」，团结所有的爆炸头，穷尽有关爆炸头的学问。
t_en: Don't know, don't care. One day, there will be a Herta Department of Curlcademy in the space station, where all the hairstyle aficionados can gather and share knowledge about the art of hairstyling.

t_ch: 待出版的集体创作著作名我已经想好了，就叫《论科员爆炸头浓度与宇宙大发现大繁荣的正函数关系》。
t_en: I have already figured out the title of the paper we will publish in collaboration: "On the positive functional relationship between the concentration of scientists with luscious hairstyles and the prosperity and great discoveries of the universe."

t_ch: 你已经<color=#dbc291ff>第四次</color>出现在我的眼前，衣服也从来没换过…罢了，现在，我想问你一个问题。
t_en: You have appeared before me for the <color=#dbc291ff>fourth</color> time, and your clothes have never been changed... Now, I want to ask you a question.

t_ch: 哼，暮气沉沉，千篇一律…所有人都穿着相同制式的衣服。
t_en: Hum! Nothing special, it's so boring here... Everyone wears the same clothes.

t_ch: 究竟要怎么样，才能从混乱的人群中一眼认出一名科员呢？
t_en: How on earth are we supposed to recognize a researcher in a crowd?

t_ch: 依靠头发辨认。
t_en: By their hair.

t_ch: 嗯，小囡之前也常常这么说，「爸爸，你烫个爆炸头吧，这样我就能从一堆同科室的科员伯伯中把你一眼认出来了」。
t_en: Well, my daughter used to say to me, "Daddy, you should get a more luscious hairstyle, then I can tell you apart from all the other old guys in your department."

t_ch: 依靠衣服辨认。
t_en: By their clothes.

t_ch: 不，这个方式不科学，小囡很早以前就发现了，「爸爸，你烫个爆炸头吧，这样我就能从一堆同科室的科员伯伯中把你一眼认出来了」。
t_en: No, this method is not scientific. My daughter worked it out a long time ago, "Daddy, you should get a more luscious hairstyle, then I can tell you apart from the other old guys in your department."

t_ch: 我之前还不相信，直到「军团」入侵的时候，她叫着我的名字，却朝着反方向跑走了——一定是见到了背影和我相似的科员。
t_en: I didn't listen to her until the Legion invaded. She called out my name, but ran off in the opposite direction — she must have seen a staff member who looked similar to me.

t_ch: 后来嘛，我终于找到了那个跟我背影相似的科员，他趴在月台上，不远处躺着小囡。
t_en: Later, I found that researcher who looked similar to me. He was lying face down on the platform. My girl was not far away.

t_ch: 我告诉她，这次爸爸一定会烫个爆炸头喔，可她的小手冰冰凉。
t_en: I told her... that Daddy will definitely get a more luscious hairstyle this time. However, her little hands had already gone cold.

t_ch: 麻烦的是，现在「无敌宇宙核弹头协会」的成员越来越多了。以后究竟还要怎么样，才能从混乱的人群中一眼认出一名科员呢？
t_en: The trouble is that there are now more and more members in the Invincible Space Warhead Association. In the future, how can we recognize a researcher in a crowd?

t_ch: 随身物都没有逃过「巴卜昂乓嘭」的命运，只剩下一些毫无印象的垃圾。
t_en: None of my belongings escaped that "Babracadabra" mishap. All that was left was a bunch of unrecognizable garbage.

t_ch: 罢了，都是身外之物，至少还有头发陪着我。
t_en: Whatever, they are merely worldly possessions anyway. At least I have my hair with me.

t_ch: 欸，又是你！没记错的话，这已经是你我的<color=#dbc291ff>第五次</color>对话。
t_en: Jeez... you again! If I am not mistaken, this is our <color=#dbc291ff>fifth</color> encounter.

t_ch: …你缠着我这么久，也算陪我解了闷，这些东西就送给你吧——我真的不是懒得靠近垃圾桶喔。
t_en: ...You've been pestering me for so long, I guess it was kind of entertaining. Here, take these — It's not because I'm too lazy to toss them out.

t_ch: 狂野爆炸头将一堆「垃圾」塞进了你的怀里，其中夹杂着一张奇怪的卡片。
t_en: The person with the luscious hairstyle shoves a pile of garbage into your arms, among them is a strange-looking card.

t_ch: 隐约可以辨认出是一张特殊权限卡——残角破碎，字面磨损，不知是否还可投入使用。
t_en: This special access card is vaguely distinguishable from the others — the corners are chipped and the writings are worn out. It's unclear if it works.

t_ch: 随着偶然的光线折射，可以发现上面印着模糊的「集团」字样。
t_en: When the light is at a certain angle, the words "Corporation" can vaguely be seen printed on the card.

t_ch: 风趣？不，还是深情一点好。文雅？嗯，俚语是不该写进诗里。
t_en: Should I make it witty? Nah, maybe sentimental is better. Elegance? Well, I probably shouldn't use idioms in a poem.

t_ch: 这里该用「黑发绸」还是「白裙裾」？嘶，头皮发麻……
t_en: Should I use "dark silk" or "white gown" here? Tsk... It's hurting my brain...
